competencies: Compute energy and mass balances for combustion firings
Calculate fuel consumption and flue gas composition for a heat demand
Assess ignition stability, flash-back and blow-off criteria of flames
Design firings for energy efficiency and reduced emissions
Evaluate explosion and detonation conditions for safety
description_en: Combustion fundamentals covering fuels, flame stability, balances and the design of energy-efficient, low-emission firings.

competencies: Explain working principles and construction of turbomachines
Select and dimension pumps, turbines and compressors
Analyse operating behaviour and control for optimal energy conversion
Apply affinity laws and dimensionless characteristic numbers
Assess energy transfer in fluid-flow machines
description_en: Fundamentals, design, operation and control of fluid-energy machines such as pumps, turbines and compressors for efficient energy conversion.

competencies: Compare biomass feedstocks and biofuels with fossil alternatives
Distinguish thermochemical and biotechnological conversion processes
Apply reaction-engineering models to biomass conversion
Conduct life-cycle assessment for biofuel production and use
Search and critically review scientific literature via Scopus
description_en: Conversion of biomass to fuels, process modelling and life-cycle assessment for sustainable biofuel production and utilisation.

competencies: Explain principles of electrochemical energy conversion
Analyse steady-state behaviour and current-voltage curves of fuel cells
Design and optimise fuel cell systems
Apply Nernst equation and reaction kinetics to fuel cells
Assess hydrogen handling, storage and fuel processing
description_en: Principles, steady-state behaviour, design and optimisation of fuel cell systems including hydrogen handling and fuel processing.

competencies: Explain fundamentals of automatic power system operation
Perform stability analysis and design power system control laws
Formulate and solve power system optimization problems
Solve economic dispatch and unit commitment problems
Compute optimal power flow and frequency/voltage control
description_en: Automatic operation, stability control and optimization of electrical power systems, including economic dispatch and optimal power flow.

competencies: Explain microbiological and metabolic fundamentals for engineers
Operate bioreactors and cultivate microorganisms
Apply measurement techniques used in bioprocess engineering
Plan, conduct and evaluate fermentation and purification experiments
Document experimental results in written protocols
description_en: Microbiological and engineering fundamentals of biotechnological processes with hands-on cultivation, bioreactor operation and product purification.

competencies: Build mathematical models of biotechnological processes
Formulate mass balances and reaction-kinetic equations for bioreactors
Solve differential model equations using numerical integration
Estimate model parameters from experimental data
Implement bioprocess models in Matlab programs
description_en: Mathematical modelling of biotechnological processes with mass balances, reaction kinetics and Matlab-based numerical solution and parameter estimation.

competencies: Explain architectures and learning rules of neural network models
Analyse stability and asymptotic behaviour of feedforward and recurrent networks
Model associative memory and excitatory-inhibitory networks
Apply reinforcement and actor-critic learning concepts
Implement and visualise neural models in small programs
description_en: Theoretical foundations of neural network architectures and learning rules, including recurrent networks, associative memory and reinforcement learning.

competencies: Describe biophysical generation of neuronal activity mathematically
Model neurons with RC, LIF and Hodgkin-Huxley equations
Analyse encoding and decoding of sensory information
Apply Bayesian estimation and information-theoretic measures
Simulate dynamical neural systems in self-written programs
description_en: Biophysical foundations of neuronal activity and neural coding, with mathematical models, Bayesian decoding and information theory.

competencies: Model cellular networks using graph and hypergraph theory
Perform metabolic network analysis with elementary modes and cut sets
Model regulatory and signalling networks with Boolean networks
Compute structural and statistical network measures
Apply software tools for computational analysis of biochemical networks
description_en: Structural and qualitative modelling and analysis of cellular networks using graph theory, Boolean networks and metabolic network methods.

competencies: Build dynamic simulation models of wastewater treatment plants
Apply activated sludge models (ASM3) to biological treatment
Calibrate and verify plant models with simulation software
Design measurement and oxygen control concepts for treatment plants
Combine steady-state design with dynamic simulation
description_en: Dynamic simulation and control of biological wastewater treatment using activated sludge models for reliable, economic plant operation.

competencies: Explain the physics and operation of computed tomography
Apply system theory of imaging systems
Implement tomographic reconstruction algorithms
Use Fourier, filtered back-projection and algebraic methods
Identify and correct image artefacts in CT
description_en: Theory and application of computed tomography, covering imaging physics and mathematical reconstruction methods with artefact correction.

competencies: Describe modalities of modern medical imaging
Explain principles of X-ray, CT, PET/SPECT, ultrasound and MRI
Assess image quality and patient risks of imaging methods
Outline data processing steps for medical images
Apply basic image processing in laboratory exercises
description_en: Introductory overview of modern medical imaging modalities, their principles, image quality, risks and required data processing.

competencies: Apply concepts for handling uncertain knowledge in modelling and decisions
Develop and parametrise Bayesian networks
Perform inference over Bayesian networks
Apply stochastic estimation theory
Use Wiener and Kalman filters for state estimation
description_en: Concepts and methods for reasoning under uncertainty, covering Bayesian networks, stochastic estimation and Wiener/Kalman filtering.

competencies: Apply stochastic models to electromagnetic compatibility problems
Model electromagnetic cable coupling in random structures
Describe reverberation chambers as stochastic test environments
Use plane-wave field descriptions and correlation functions
Interpret and evaluate EMC measurement results
description_en: Application of stochastic models to electromagnetic compatibility, modelling cable coupling and reverberation-chamber test environments.

competencies: Build mathematical models of physiological systems from physical laws
Model the cardiovascular system and cell electrical properties
Model signal transmission and processing in nerve cells and senses
Simulate physiological systems with computer tools
Transfer modelling methods to related research questions
description_en: Mathematical modelling and computer simulation of selected physiological systems based on physical-chemical laws.

competencies: Characterise properties of stochastic processes
Derive and approximate master equations via Fokker-Planck equations
Relate stochastic differential equations to Fokker-Planck descriptions
Simulate stochastic processes with the Gillespie algorithm
Model biophysical non-equilibrium systems stochastically
description_en: Introduction to stochastic modelling of random processes in biophysical non-equilibrium systems, with analysis and numerical simulation.

competencies: Explain structure and function of embedded systems in mechatronics
Design analog solutions based on operational amplifiers
Design digital solutions based on microcontrollers
Specify and generate real-time software
Implement functions on programmable logic
description_en: Fundamentals of embedded mechatronic systems covering analog and digital solutions, signal processing and real-time behaviour.

competencies: Develop hardware and software for embedded mechatronic systems
Program 8-bit and 32-bit microcontrollers with real-time operating systems
Specify functions with Matlab/Simulink and ASCET and generate code
Apply AUTOSAR and ECU architectures for automotive control units
Implement real-time control on industrial PCs and mobile devices
description_en: Advanced development of embedded mechatronic hardware and software for automotive ECUs, industrial PCs and mobile devices using modern tools.

competencies: Formulate mechatronic design problems
Apply the V-model of systems development
Design open and feedback-controlled action chains
Analyse parameter sensitivity of mechatronic systems
Apply optimization to mechatronic system design
description_en: Methodical design of mechatronic systems using the V-model, functional design, design tools and system optimization.

competencies: Formulate mechatronic design problems for sensor-actuator systems
Apply the V-model with hierarchical, component-based design
Perform integrated mechatronic system design
Assess parameter sensitivity and robustness
Apply fundamentals of system optimization
description_en: Advanced integrated design of mechatronic sensor-actuator systems using component-based methods, robustness analysis and optimization.

competencies: Describe kinematics of serial robotic manipulators
Analyse rigid-body and instantaneous kinematics
Derive the dynamics of robotic systems
Simulate robotic systems on a computer
Model and analyse robot manipulators mathematically
description_en: Foundations of kinematics, dynamics and computer simulation of robotic manipulators and serial robot systems.

competencies: Model electromechanical systems using Lagrange equations
Explain construction and behaviour of piezo and magnetostrictive actuators
Design power amplifiers and control for piezo actuators
Integrate electric actuators into complex mechanical systems
Apply actuators to vibration damping and positioning
description_en: Construction, behaviour and control of electromechanical actuators such as piezo and magnetostrictive actuators and their system integration.

competencies: Explain tasks and application fields of programmable logic controllers
Design binary control circuits for asynchronous machines
Program PLC-controlled drive systems
Implement machine and plant control with PLCs
Control motion-control systems with stored-program drive control
description_en: Structure and programming of programmable logic controllers for binary control and drive automation, with hands-on programming exercises.

competencies: Design, model and control robot drive systems
Describe spatial robot motion with Denavit-Hartenberg coordinates
Build dynamic models of robotic systems
Program robot controllers and cascade drive control
Conduct experimental investigations on robot systems
description_en: Design, modelling, control and programming of robot drive systems, including dynamic models and experimental robot investigations.

competencies: Design and build distributed digital automation systems
Plan integration of diverse automation components
Apply automation and information technologies in process control
Interpret abstract automation and information models
Develop functional automation systems
description_en: Design and construction of distributed digital automation systems, integrating automation and information technologies for process control.

competencies: Analyse industrial communication systems using the ISO/OSI model
Explain principles of industrial communication protocols and services
Configure Ethernet/TCP/IP systems
Describe industrial bus systems such as PROFIBUS and CAN
Classify security aspects of industrial communication
description_en: Industrial communication systems based on the ISO/OSI model, covering protocols, Ethernet/TCP/IP, fieldbuses and security.

competencies: Explain construction and operation of automation devices
Describe working principles of digital measurement and actuating devices
Explain pneumatic and hydraulic actuating devices
Apply automation devices in practical exercises
Interpret realisation forms with different signal and energy carriers
description_en: Construction, operation and interconnection of automation devices, including electrical, pneumatic and hydraulic sensors and actuators.

competencies: Design hardware and software platforms for embedded systems
Model embedded systems with extended finite state machines
Apply object-oriented design to embedded software development
Realise functions on FPGA and DSP platforms
Validate embedded systems through testing
description_en: Architectures, design and implementation of computer-controlled embedded systems on hardware and software platforms, with formal methods and testing.

competencies: Explain methods and techniques of image and video coding
Assess existing coding schemes for still and moving images
Estimate the information content of images
Develop encoders for image and video compression
Apply transform and semantic coding techniques
description_en: Methods and techniques of image and video coding, from signal-theoretic to semantic approaches, for compression and communication.

competencies: Explain information content, entropy and redundancy
Apply source coding with Shannon-Fano and Huffman methods
Compute channel capacity and channel entropies
Design linear block and cyclic codes for channel coding
Apply syndrome decoding and error-correcting methods
description_en: Foundations of information and coding theory covering entropy, channel capacity, source coding and error-correcting channel codes.

competencies: Solve a research problem independently as an individual or team
Acquire subject-specific skills needed to tackle a problem
Decompose a problem into manageable sub-problems
Derive and document a suitable scientific solution
Present and defend results in a colloquium
description_en: Team or individual research project in which students independently solve a scientific problem and present the results in a colloquium.

competencies: Conduct independent scientific research on a current topic
Apply modern research methods to plan and execute studies
Evaluate experimental or theoretical results
Document results in a scientific written thesis
Present and defend the thesis before an expert audience
description_en: Independent master's thesis applying modern research methods to a current scientific topic, documented and defended in scientific form.
